metadata.name: ww-kanban
metadata.description: A Kanban board element that groups item data into draggable stacks and renders each stack through a ww-stack child.
features: Groups item objects into stacks by a configured item property
Renders each stack with a ww-stack dependency
Supports manual or bound stack definitions
Sortable cards within and between stacks
Optional uncategorized stack for unmatched items
Ascending or descending sorting inside stacks
Item moved event with source and destination stack values
Readonly isDragging variable while any stack item is being dragged

properties.name: wrapStacks
properties.description: Whether stacks wrap to a new row when there is not enough horizontal space.

properties.name: items
properties.description: Array or collection of item objects displayed as cards in the board.

properties.name: itemKey
properties.description: Object property path used as the stable unique key for each item. It must reference a property that exists on every item, for example "id", "slug", or "['id']".

properties.name: stackedBy
properties.description: Object property path on each item that determines which stack it belongs to, for example "status" or "['status']".

properties.name: sortedBy
properties.description: Optional object property path used to sort items inside each stack.

properties.name: sortOrder
properties.description: Sort direction for items inside each stack when sortedBy is set.

properties.name: sortable
properties.description: Whether cards can be dragged and reordered.

properties.name: uncategorizedStack
properties.description: Whether to display an extra stack containing items whose stackedBy value does not match any configured stack value.

properties.name: stacks
properties.description: Array or collection of stack objects. Each stack must provide a label and value, or matching custom paths through stackLabel and stackValue.

properties.name: stackLabel
properties.description: Object property path used as the display label for each stack when stacks are object data.

properties.name: stackValue
properties.description: Object property path used as the stack value when stacks are object data. Item stackedBy values must match this value.

properties.name: readonly
properties.description: Whether all drag-and-drop interactions are disabled.

properties.name: draggingCursor
properties.description: CSS cursor used globally while an item is being dragged.

properties.name: customDragHandle
properties.description: Whether dragging must start from an element inside the card that has the handleClass CSS class.

properties.name: handleClass
properties.description: CSS class used as the drag handle when customDragHandle is true.

states.name: readonly
states.description: Applied when the Kanban board is readonly and cannot be reordered.

events.name: item:moved
events.description: Triggered when a card is moved within the same stack or into another stack.

payload.oldIndex: previous item index, or null when added into a stack
payload.updatedList: array of items in the updated destination stack

slots.name: stackElement
slots.description: Required ww-stack child rendered once for each configured stack. Its itemElement slot defines the card template and its headerElement slot defines the stack header.

variables.name: isDragging
variables.description: True while any card in the Kanban board is being dragged.

specifications: Always include a stackElement slot containing a ww-stack child. Without it, the Kanban has no stack UI.
Configure the ww-stack child with headerElement and itemElement slots. The ww-kanban passes each stack's items and metadata into the ww-stack automatically through ww-props.
The items property must resolve to an array of objects. Each item must include a stable unique field and a stack/category field such as status.

When items come from a collection or table view, set itemKey to an existing unique field returned by that data source. Do not assume id exists; if the rows expose slug, uuid, or another unique field, use that field instead.
Set stackedBy to the item field that stores the stack value. Each item value must match one of the stacks values.
The stacks property must resolve to an array of objects. By default each stack object uses label for display and value for matching items.
Use stackLabel and stackValue only when the stack objects use custom field names instead of label and value.
Inside the ww-stack headerElement slot, context.item.data is the current stack object with label, value, and items fields.
Inside the ww-stack itemElement slot, context.item.data is the current card/item object.
Do not add a repeat to the stackElement slot or to the ww-stack itemElement slot; repetition is handled by ww-kanban and ww-stack.
Use uncategorizedStack only when unmatched items should remain visible.
Set readonly to true for display-only boards.
If customDragHandle is true, add the handleClass value as an HTML class on a child element inside the card template.
Use the item:moved event to persist changes to variables, collections, or a backend. The component does not automatically mutate external source data beyond its internal display order.

examples.description: Project Kanban board with three stacks and repeated cards.
Readonly Kanban bound to a table view data array.
